title: Choosing the Right Model Size: When Bigger Isn't Better
author: AI Systems Engineering Team
excerpt: GPT-4 is impressive, but do you need it? Often, a smaller model is faster, cheaper, and good enough.

content.text: There's a natural instinct to use the biggest, most capable model available. GPT-4 for everything! But bigger models cost more, run slower, and aren't always necessary. Smart model selection is a key engineering skill.
Ask these questions before choosing a model:

items: Is the task complex reasoning or simple classification?
What's the latency requirement (real-time chat vs batch processing)?
Do we need the model to follow complex instructions?
Is the output safety-critical (medical, legal, financial)?
Small models (7B params): Classification, sentiment, extraction. Cheap and fast.
Medium models (13-30B params): Summarization, translation, simple Q&A. Good balance.
Large models (70B+ params): Complex reasoning, coding, creative writing. Use sparingly.
Frontier models (GPT-4, Claude 3): When accuracy is critical and cost is secondary.

content.text: A 7B parameter model running locally costs ~$0.0001 per 1K tokens. GPT-4 costs ~$0.03 per 1K tokens. For a customer support bot handling 1M tokens/day, that's $30 vs $3,000 per month — a 100x difference.
The best approach: start small, measure accuracy, and upgrade only when the task demands it. Your CFO will thank you.
